compiled_state.title: Tier E E4: port init / fsck / fsck-recover in-process (highest-risk bootstrap+recovery; isolated soak each)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: PRIOR-ART RATIONALE for the auto-init consent gate (user-requested record). Git-attached trackers fall in two camps: (1) silent implicit creation via ref-only storage — git-bug (refs/bugs/*, first 'git bug add' auto-creates), git-appraise (refs/notes) — no init, invisible footprint; (2) explicit init required — git-issue/dspinellis ('git issue init' creates .issues/), bugs-everywhere/sit ('be init'/'sit init'), Fossil (own SQLite, 'fossil new'). rebar sits in camp 2 and is MORE conservative than git-bug: explicit 'rebar init'/init_repo() OR interactive-confirmed auto-init ([Y/n] default Yes), NEVER silent in non-interactive (CI/pipe/library/MCP -> error). Justification: rebar's init mutates the WORKING TREE (orphan 'tickets' branch + linked worktree at .tickets-tracker/ + .git/info/exclude edits + .env-id/.closure-key), a heavier/visible footprint than git-bug's invisible refs — so silent materialization on a stray read would surprise; requiring consent matches the weight of the operation. Stricter-than-most point: rebar ERRORS on non-interactive auto-init rather than defaulting to create (git-bug would create; git-issue errors with no auto-init at all). Source: docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md (benchmarks git-bug/git-issue/Fossil/dstask) + maintainer knowledge (git-bug/git-issue/Fossil high-confidence; dstask/ticgit specifics unverified).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: E4 COMPLETE — init/fsck/fsck-recover all in-process + auto-init consent gate. Commits: fsck (56e130ff), fsck-recover (07d7b495), init+gate (a9c4dd64). AC: init bootstrap ported (orphan branch/worktree, recovery, .gitignore/.env-id/.closure-key, lock, symlink case, idempotency) with byte-parity messages + identical tickets-branch tree; fsck (5 checks, text+json transform) + fsck-recover (rebase/cherry-pick recovery, exit 0/1/2/3) byte-parity; library init_repo/fsck rewired off subprocess. Consent gate: never inits without interactive [Y/n] confirmation or explicit init/init_repo() — non-interactive errors, validated for CLI(TTY+no-TTY)/library/MCP. Full suite 1714 passed; concurrency matrix green. Legacy bash dispatcher keeps silent auto-init (E7-deleted).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
